entry.title: Washington Post: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Putin threatens new targets if Ukraine gets longer-range rockets; missiles hit Kyiv
  • Putin threatens to hit new targets if long-range missiles are sent to Ukraine
  • Queen Elizabeth II makes surprise appearance for final day of jubilee
  • The big lunch: Britain celebrates the queen’s jubilee with picnic spreads
  • Striking moments from Queen Elizabeth II’s Platinum Jubilee
  • The queen and Paddington Bear light up a rocking palace
  • She lost her house to the rising sea. Nowhere else feels like home.
  • Vigilantes and violence have migrants in South Africa scared for their lives
  • As war drags on, weary Russians yearn for a return to normal life
  • Canadian singer Jacob Hoggard convicted of sexual assault
  • And she waved: Festive pageant caps queen's Platinum Jubilee
  • Pakistan army says 7 militants killed near Afghan border
  • UN: Yemen's warring sides resume talks on ending Taiz siege
  • Bolsonaro using medals to celebrate allies, family, himself
  • 2 Jordanian Air Force pilots killed in plane crash
  • At least 49 dead in 2nd day of Bangladesh cargo depot fire
  • Philippine volcano spews ash and steam, alarms villagers
  • Death toll in Iran building collapse rises to at least 38
  • Greek firefighters battle blaze near Athens for second day
  • 10 dead in shootings in Philadelphia, Tennessee, S. Carolina and Michigan
  • There have been over 200 mass shootings so far in 2022
  • Ex-judge killed in ‘targeted act’ against judicial system, state says
  • After the shooting, Uvalde becomes a new stop on a grim American circuit
  • Beat cancer? Your Medicare Advantage plan might still be billing for it.
  • The Long-Lasting Legacy of a Short-Term Prime Minister
  • Western ‘Unity’ Is Making the Ukraine War Worse
  • Foxconn’s EV Push Takes It Back to the Future
  • The Barbarians Are At the Gate. Don’t Resist Them
  • How China Is Reviving Tools for Hedging Credit Risk
  • ‘Fake’ Aluminum Stocks Put Perils of China’s Commodities Funding in Spotlight
  • Here’s How US States Differ on Carrying Guns in Public
  • Why ‘NOPEC’ Keeps Arising as a U.S. Answer to OPEC
  • This is no time to hesitate in Ukraine
  • The Jan. 6 committee has a narrow but priceless opening
  • The Supreme Court gives tech a win — and a reason to worry
  • The Post endorses Jim Shalleck in the GOP primary for Maryland attorney general
  • The U.S. can’t get the hemisphere together because it’s coming apart
  • Democrats should be clear: A radical GOP is holding the country back
  • Who was the biggest threat to Mike Pence on Jan. 6, 2021?
  • Cartoon by Drew Sheneman
  • For some Afghan refugees, there is no safety net
  • Acting now on climate will be less painful
  • The difficulties in dealing with inflation
  • Patents protect people’s access to life-saving medicine
  • It’s past time for a ban on assault weapons
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 6 Jun, 2022

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 6 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-06 03:07AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • LME faces Elliott International’s US$456 million law suit for suspending nickel futures trading
  • Wear masks for long term to reduce antibiotics use, University of Hong Kong researchers say
  • Can John Lee learn from Carrie Lam’s mistakes and rebuild public trust?
  • Over 900 Chinese antiques, art pieces to be displayed in Hong Kong Palace Museum, including national treasures spanning 5,000 years
  • Hong Kong advocates call for better protection of pre-war building once used for spying against Japanese during World War II
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong logs 515 cases, clusters tied to bars in Central grow as authorities warn of rising infections
  • Amnesty International slams Hong Kong police for arresting 6 people over efforts to mark Tiananmen crackdown
  • ATM, online banking services in Hong Kong resume for millions of HSBC, Hang Seng customers after brief disruption

  • Afghanistan appeals to China to help put out raging Nuristan forest fire
  • Why Taiwan fans are cheering Tom Cruise’s return as Top Gun star
  • Chinese aerospace firm files patent for faster, more stable way to launch military drones
  • Jack Ma’s Ant Group unveils Singapore digital bank – ANEXT – in overseas expansion push
  • Australia-China relations: Albanese reaches out to Beijing over fighter jet encounter
  • How rising India’s Quad role is helping it find its place among global powers
  • US, South Korea launch eight missiles in response to North Korea missile firings
  • Accident or negligence? US naval officer in Japan faces prison over deadly crash – family claims mountain sickness
  • Philippine volcano spews ash and steam, in ‘abnormal conditions’
  • Cambodians face intimidation, threats as they head to polls for local election
  • How an Afghan woman who fled the Taliban is fighting for others like her
  • Massive blaze at Bangladesh container depot kills at least 49, injures more than 300
  • Singapore taps Australia, Thailand, Brazil for chicken after Malaysia’s export ban
  • Vietnam’s child drowning plague, and the community swimming trainers doing all they can to end it
  • North Korea unleashes barrage of missiles in response to US military drills
  • Embattled UK Prime Minister Boris Johnson could face leadership challenge this week
  • Can Joe Biden convince the US’ Middle East allies he still has their back?
  • Ukraine claims its forces control ‘half’ of Sievierodonetsk against Russia
  • UK government accused of trying to deport unaccompanied minors to Rwanda
  • Gulf states condemn Indian official’s ‘Islamophobic’ remarks about the Prophet Mohammed
  • Ethiopia accuses aid agencies of delivering banned equipment to Tigray that could be used by rebels
  • ‘Evil’: At least 50 killed in massacre at Catholic Church in southwest Nigeria
  • UK’s Queen Elizabeth ends Platinum Jubilee with surprise appearance
  • Nine dead, dozens wounded in weekend US mass shootings
  • New breast cancer drug could help thousands, researchers say
  • Pop superstar Ed Sheeran to crown Queen Elizabeth’s four-day jubilee jamboree
  • Russia hits Kyiv with missiles; Putin warns West about supplies
  • As Ukraine loses more and more troops, how long can it keep up the fight?
  • Nato chief in push to overcome Turkish opposition to Finland, Sweden joining
  • Russian missiles strike Ukraine capital Kyiv after weeks of relative calm
  • Chinese stocks jump in Hong Kong as Beijing rolls back Covid-19 curbs amid recovery signs while HKEX wobbles on LME lawsuit
  • Ant Group launches digital banking service in Singapore to serve small firms doing international trades
  • Hong Kong should stick to four-decade-old US dollar currency peg as it braces for ‘unusual challenges’ ahead, HKMA former CEO Yam says
  • Europe’s top money manager Amundi lends weight to Chinese stocks as market looks for sustained rebound
  • Like all wars, the US tech war on China is proving costly and pointless
  • Why we’ve not seen the end of stock market corrections or inflation
  • Climate change: How Chinese shipbuilding giant CSSC’s floating wind turbine will help advance the country’s decarbonisation drive
  • Global struggle against climate change must be fought – and won – in the Asia-Pacific
  • US unicorn Deel wants to ‘revolutionise’ HR function in Hong Kong, help firms hire and pay remote contractors and employees
  • Developing tomorrow’s tech talents can fuel Hong Kong’s ambitions to become the next Silicon Valley
  • China gaming ban: no new licenses in May points to ‘new normal’ in regulatory hostility
  • Top shopping influencer Li Jiaqi a no-show at scheduled live stream after abrupt end to session on Friday
  • Ancient Chinese porn served as sex education and was even used for fire prevention
  • Louis Vuitton, Rolex and Tag Heuer at 2022 Monaco Grand Prix highlight how luxury brands and sports have never been closer
  • Luxurious Urban Living in a Tranquil Hong Kong Haven
  • In City of Orange, YA writer David Yoon’s adult novel, unexplained desolation gives way to incapacitating heartbreak
  • K-drama Eve: Seo Ye-ji’s comeback, two years after It’s Okay to Not Be Okay, is standard high-society fare
  • What is microdosing? Using psychedelic drugs in tiny amounts regularly enhances your mood and creativity, its proponents argue. What does the science say?
  • What to wear to a wedding in 2022 – fashion experts’ tips for women and men on the best attire
  • The history of Hong Kong’s Fire Services Department, and the devastating infernos that forced the city to form a proper response force
  • 7 Indian LGBT icons you need to know: from YouTuber Lilly Singh, Olympian Dutee Chand and gay royal Prince Manvendra Singh Gohil, to transgender activist Laxmi Narayan Tripathi and film director Onir
  • Inside Khloé Kardashian’s multi-million dollar car collection: from Porsche and Ferrari supercars to Lamborghini and Rolls-Royce SUVs to rival Kim – but does Kylie Jenner outshine them both?
  • Who’s the world’s richest DJ in 2022? Net worths, ranked: from Calvin Harris’ hits for Rihanna and Dua Lipa, and Steve Aoki’s NFTs, to Daft Punk’s retirement and David Guetta’s 50 million album sales
  • How are NFTs affecting high jewellery investments? Metaverse marketplace OpenSea sells diamonds while Sotheby’s auctioned earrings inspired by a Van Gogh artwork – with accompanying digital token
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Work to create Osaka Expo site threatens wild birds on island
  • Tohoku’s only bullfighting event celebrates 40th season
  • COVID-19 cases in Tokyo fall for 23rd day, but 2 related deaths
  • Chinese exiles meet in Tokyo to mark Tiananmen anniversary
  • Tokyo to resume travel subsidy deal for residents from June 10
  • Morning exercise deemed threat to national treasure Hikone Castle
  • Brewery gets the jump with craft beer made from crickets
  • Japan blasts North Korea for missile salvo, warns of action
  • China launches mission to complete space station assembly
  • Australia says China threatened plane over South China Sea
  • North Korea test-fires salvo of short-range missiles
  • U.S., S. Korean navies end key exercise amid N. Korea tension
  • Philippine volcano spews ash and steam, alarms villagers
  • Hundreds gather to mark Tiananmen anniversary in Taiwan
  • Russia says Kyiv strikes destroyed tanks donated by West
  • Putin warns West: Russia will strike harder if longer-range missiles supplied
  • Russian missiles strike Kyiv, shattering sense of calm
  • Footprints of monsters, energy beam celebrate ‘Shin Ultraman’
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 5 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
